structure.title: Preface
structure.summary:
# Description of Best Buy Co., Inc. Form 10-Q for Quarter Ended July 29, 2023

## Main Points Covered:

**Document Type and Filing Information:**
- Quarterly Report (Form 10-Q) filed with the SEC for the quarter ended July 29, 2023
- Best Buy Co., Inc., a Minnesota corporation with headquarters in Richfield, Minnesota
- Common stock (BBY) traded on the New York Stock Exchange
- Large accelerated filer status

**Financial Statements Included:**
- Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets comparing July 29, 2023, January 28, 2023, and July 30, 2022
- Condensed Consolidated Statements of Earnings for three and six-month periods
- Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income
- Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows
- Condensed Consolidated Statements of Changes in Shareholders' Equity
- Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements

**Key Balance Sheet Highlights (as of July 29, 2023):**
- Total assets: $15.3 billion
- Current assets: $8.3 billion (including $1.1 billion cash, $5.7 billion inventory)
- Total liabilities: $12.5 billion
- Total shareholders' equity: $2.8 billion
- Outstanding shares: 217.6 million

**Management Reporting Sections:**
- Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
- Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk
- Controls and Procedures
- Legal Proceedings and Other Information

**Corporate Disclosure Channels:**
- References to investor relations website and corporate communications platforms for material information distribution

structure.title: Part I — Financial Information
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is a Form 10-Q quarterly report for Best Buy Co., Inc. for the fiscal period ended July 29, 2023. The main points covered include:

## Financial Statements and Performance
- Condensed consolidated balance sheets, statements of earnings, comprehensive income, and cash flows for the six-month period
- Revenue of $19.1 billion (down 9.2% year-over-year) with comparable sales decline of 8.2%
- Net earnings of $518 million with diluted EPS of $2.36
- Detailed segment performance for Domestic and International operations

## Key Business Metrics
- Comparable sales declines across most product categories, particularly appliances (down 16.1%) and computing/mobile phones (down 6.4%)
- Digital sales comprising 31% of Domestic revenue
- Store count changes including closures of large format stores and expansion of outlet centers
- Membership program changes and improved margin performance

## Financial Condition and Liquidity
- Cash and cash equivalents of $1.1 billion
- Total assets of $15.3 billion
- Long-term debt of $1.1 billion with no near-term maturities
- Operating cash flow of $181 million for the six-month period
- Capital expenditure expectations of approximately $850 million for fiscal 2024

## Strategic Initiatives and Outlook
- Store portfolio optimization with planned closures and remodels
- Expansion into new product categories (wellness technology, electric vehicles, outdoor living)
- Operating model adjustments to align labor costs with omnichannel strategy
- Expectations for continued industry sales pressure due to macroeconomic headwinds

## Accounting and Compliance Matters
- Adoption of new accounting standards for supplier finance program disclosures
- Restructuring charges related to employee termination benefits
- Non-GAAP financial measures reconciliations
- Disclosure controls and procedures assessment

nodes.title: Item 1. Financial Statements
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is a Form 10-Q quarterly report for Best Buy Co., Inc. covering the first six months of fiscal 2024 (ended July 29, 2023). The main points covered include:

## Financial Statements
- **Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets** showing total assets of $15.3 billion as of July 29, 2023, with current assets of $8.3 billion and total equity of $2.8 billion
- **Condensed Consolidated Statements of Earnings** reporting revenue of $19.1 billion for the six-month period, with net earnings of $518 million and diluted earnings per share of $2.36
- **Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows** indicating operating cash flow of $181 million and a decrease in cash of $762 million for the six-month period
- **Condensed Consolidated Statements of Changes in Shareholders' Equity** tracking equity movements including dividends paid ($403 million) and share repurchases ($160 million)

## Notes to Financial Statements
Key disclosures include:
- **Restructuring charges** of $16 million related to a fiscal 2023 cost optimization initiative
- **Goodwill and intangible assets** with no impairment charges recorded
- **Fair value measurements** of financial instruments and derivative instruments
- **Debt information** including $1.15 billion in long-term debt and a $1.25 billion revolving credit facility
- **Revenue recognition** policies and contract liabilities of $1.3 billion
- **Earnings per share** calculations and **share repurchase program** activity ($150 million repurchased)
- **Segment information** showing Domestic revenue of $17.7 billion and International revenue of $1.4 billion

## Management's Discussion and Analysis
- Overview of Best Buy's business segments (Domestic and International)
- Discussion of seasonal business patterns and comparable sales metrics
- Explanation of the company's purpose and strategic approach

nodes.title: Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets as of July 29, 2023, January 28, 2023, and July 30, 2022
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains the financial information section of Best Buy Co., Inc.'s interim financial statements, specifically including:

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets** - Presents the company's financial position as of three dates (July 29, 2023, January 28, 2023, and July 30, 2022), showing:
   - Current assets totaling approximately $8.3 billion, primarily comprised of merchandise inventories and receivables
   - Total assets of approximately $15.3 billion
   - Current liabilities of approximately $8.4 billion, with accounts payable being the largest component
   - Total shareholders' equity of approximately $2.8 billion
   - Long-term debt and operating lease liabilities

2. **Condensed Consolidated Statements of Earnings** - Reports financial performance for three and six-month periods ended July 29, 2023 and July 30, 2022, including:
   - Revenue comparison showing a decline from $10.3 billion to $9.6 billion (three-month period)
   - Gross profit, operating income, and net earnings metrics
   - Earnings per share data (both basic and diluted)
   - Year-over-year performance comparisons indicating decreased profitability

The document presents unaudited interim financial statements with comparative periods to show financial trends and performance changes.

nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements for a company, comparing performance across two periods:

## Main Points Covered:

**1. Condensed Consolidated Statements of Earnings**
- Presents financial performance for three-month and six-month periods ended July 29, 2023 and July 30, 2022
- Shows revenue decline: $9.6 billion (Q2 2023) vs. $10.3 billion (Q2 2022) and $19.1 billion (H1 2023) vs. $21.0 billion (H1 2022)
- Details cost of sales, gross profit, and operating expenses
- Includes restructuring charges and other income/expense items
- Reports net earnings of $274 million (Q2 2023) vs. $306 million (Q2 2022) and $518 million (H1 2023) vs. $647 million (H1 2022)
- Provides earnings per share metrics (both basic and diluted)
- Shows weighted-average common shares outstanding

**2. Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income**
- Reconciles net earnings to comprehensive income
- Includes foreign currency translation adjustments
- Comprehensive income of $279 million (Q2 2023) vs. $306 million (Q2 2022) and $518 million (H1 2023) vs. $646 million (H1 2022)

**3. Key Financial Metrics**
- Operating income decreased year-over-year
- Income tax expense and interest expense details provided
- Equity in income of affiliates included in calculations

nodes.title: Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income for the three and six months ended July 29, 2023, and July 30, 2022
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains two key financial statements from an unaudited interim financial report:

## 1. Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income
- Presents financial performance for three-month and six-month periods ended July 29, 2023 and July 30, 2022
- Shows net earnings of $274 million and $306 million for Q2 2023 and Q2 2022 respectively
- Reports six-month net earnings of $518 million (2023) versus $647 million (2022), indicating a year-over-year decline
- Includes foreign currency translation adjustments with minimal impact on comprehensive income
- Comprehensive income totals $279 million and $518 million for the respective periods

## 2. Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows
- Details cash movements for the six-month periods ended July 29, 2023 and July 30, 2022
- **Operating Activities**: Generated $181 million in cash (2023) compared to negative $709 million (2022), showing significant improvement
- **Investing Activities**: Used $381 million (2023) versus $484 million (2022), primarily for property and equipment additions
- **Financing Activities**: Used $560 million (2023) versus $861 million (2022), including stock repurchases and dividend payments
- Shows overall cash decrease of $762 million in 2023 versus $2,053 million in 2022
- Ending cash position of $1,491 million (2023) compared to $1,152 million (2022)

nodes.title: Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows for the six months ended July 29, 2023, and July 30, 2022
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains two key financial statements for a company covering the six-month periods ended July 29, 2023 and July 30, 2022:

## Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows

**Main Points:**
- **Operating Activities**: Net earnings of $518M (2023) vs. $647M (2022), with adjustments including depreciation/amortization (~$473M), stock-based compensation ($75M), and changes in working capital. Operating cash flow improved to $181M in 2023 from negative $709M in 2022, primarily due to better management of receivables and accounts payable.
- **Investing Activities**: Capital expenditures of $395M (2023) vs. $441M (2022), with a net proceeds of $14M from subsidiary sale in 2023.
- **Financing Activities**: Total cash used of $560M (2023) vs. $861M (2022), including stock repurchases ($158M vs. $465M) and dividend payments (~$402M in both periods).
- **Overall**: Cash decreased by $762M in 2023 vs. $2,053M in 2022, ending at $1,491M.

## Condensed Consolidated Statements of Changes in Shareholders' Equity

**Main Points:**
- Tracks changes in shareholders' equity components across multiple periods (Q1 and six-month periods for 2023 and 2022)
- Shows common shares outstanding declining from 227.4M (Jan 2022) to 217.9M (July 2023) due to repurchases
- Total shareholders' equity at July 29, 2023 was $2,835M
- Key equity movements include net earnings, stock-based compensation, dividend payments, and share repurchases
- Retained earnings increased from $2,430M to $2,491M in the six-month 2023 period despite dividend payments

nodes.title: Condensed Consolidated Statements of Changes in Shareholders' Equity for the three and six months ended July 29, 2023, and July 30, 2022
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document from Best Buy Co., Inc.'s quarterly financial report contains the following main points:

## Condensed Consolidated Statements of Changes in Shareholders' Equity
- Presents detailed tracking of shareholders' equity changes for three periods: three and six months ended July 29, 2023, and comparative periods from 2022
- Shows movements in common shares, common stock, additional paid-in capital, retained earnings, and accumulated other comprehensive income
- Key transactions tracked include net earnings, stock-based compensation, stock issuances, dividend payments, and share repurchases
- Total shareholders' equity at July 29, 2023 was $2,835 million

## Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements
- **Basis of Presentation**: Confirms GAAP compliance and notes the seasonal nature of the business with significant revenue concentrated in the fourth quarter
- **Sale of Subsidiary**: Documents the completion of a Mexico subsidiary sale in Q2 fiscal 2024, generating a $21 million gain
- **Adopted Accounting Pronouncements**: Details adoption of ASU 2022-04 regarding supplier finance program disclosures
- **Supply Chain Financing**: Describes a supply chain financing program with an independent financial institution, with outstanding obligations of $609 million as of July 29, 2023
- **Cash Reconciliation**: Provides reconciliation of cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash totaling $1,491 million as of July 29, 2023
- **Reclassifications**: Notes minor reclassifications made to cash flow statements for consistency

nodes.title: Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains the Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements and Management's Discussion and Analysis from Best Buy Co., Inc.'s Form 10-Q filing for the first six months of fiscal 2024 (ended July 29, 2023).

## Main Points Covered:

**Financial Statement Notes:**
- **Basis of Presentation**: Interim financial statements with normal recurring adjustments; seasonal business with significant Q4 revenue
- **Sale of Subsidiary**: $21 million gain recognized from Mexico subsidiary sale
- **Accounting Pronouncements**: Adoption of ASU 2022-04 regarding supplier finance program disclosures
- **Supply Chain Financing**: $609 million in supplier finance obligations outstanding
- **Cash and Restricted Cash**: Total of $1,491 million as of July 29, 2023
- **Restructuring**: Fiscal 2023 resource optimization initiative with $129 million cumulative charges for employee termination benefits
- **Goodwill and Intangible Assets**: $1,383 million net goodwill; definite-lived intangible assets of $148 million net
- **Fair Value Measurements**: Recurring fair value measurements of financial assets; long-term debt fair value of $991 million
- **Derivative Instruments**: Foreign exchange hedges and interest rate swaps totaling $652 million notional amount
- **Debt**: $1.25 billion revolving credit facility; $1,150 million in long-term notes outstanding
- **Revenue**: Contract balances including gift cards, deferred revenue, and membership liabilities
- **Earnings Per Share**: Basic EPS of $1.25 and diluted EPS of $1.25 for Q2 fiscal 2024
- **Share Repurchases**: $150 million spent on 2.0 million shares in first half of fiscal 2024
- **Segments**: Domestic and International segments with revenue breakdown by product category

**Management's Discussion and Analysis:**
- Company overview and strategic purpose
- Segment structure (Domestic and International)
- Seasonal business nature with Q4 holiday emphasis
- Comparable sales metric definition and methodology

nodes.title: Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is Best Buy Co., Inc.'s Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A) section from a quarterly report (Form 10-Q), covering the second quarter and first six months of fiscal 2024 (ended July 29, 2023).

## Main Points Covered:

**Financial Overview:**
- Total assets decreased from $15.8 billion (January 28, 2023) to $15.3 billion (July 29, 2023)
- Revenue declined 7.2% in Q2 and 9.2% in the first six months compared to prior year
- Comparable sales declined 6.2% in Q2 and 8.2% in the first six months

**Business Segments:**
- Two reportable segments: Domestic (U.S. operations and Best Buy Health) and International (Canada)
- Domestic segment revenue of $8.9 billion in Q2, down 7.1% year-over-year
- International segment revenue of $693 million in Q2, down 8.8% year-over-year

**Operational Performance:**
- Digital sales comprised 31% of Domestic revenue, consistent with prior year
- Gross profit rates improved due to favorable product margins and membership program changes
- Operating income decreased due to lower sales volume and fixed expense leverage
- Store portfolio adjustments: planning to close 20-30 large format stores and expand Outlet stores to approximately 25

**Key Business Initiatives:**
- Membership program restructuring with lower cost-to-serve model
- Omnichannel strategy focusing on store experience and digital integration
- Expansion into new categories: wellness technology, electric transportation, outdoor living, and EV charging
- Labor model optimization balancing cost efficiency with employee flexibility

**Financial Condition & Liquidity:**
- Cash and cash equivalents of $1.1 billion as of July 29, 2023
- $1.25 billion revolving credit facility with no outstanding borrowings
- Debt of $1.15 billion in senior unsecured notes
- Share repurchase program of $5.0 billion authorized with no expiration date
- Regular quarterly dividend of $0.92 per share

**Market Risks & Challenges:**
- Macroeconomic headwinds including inflation and rising interest rates
- Consumer electronics industry decline from pandemic demand pull-forward
- Foreign currency exchange rate impacts, particularly Canadian dollar weakness
- Exposure to interest rate and foreign currency risks

**Non-GAAP Measures:**
- Reconciliations provided for operating income, effective tax rate, and diluted EPS adjustments
- Adjustments include intangible asset amortization, restructuring charges, and investment losses

nodes.title: Item 3. Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document excerpt from a Form 10-Q filing covers three main regulatory disclosure sections:

## Item 3: Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk
- **Interest Rate Risk**: Details the company's exposure to short-term interest rate fluctuations through cash holdings ($1.5 billion) and floating-rate debt ($0.5 billion). Provides sensitivity analysis showing that a 50-basis point rate change would impact interest income by approximately $5 million.
- **Foreign Currency Exchange Rate Risk**: Describes exposure to foreign exchange fluctuations, primarily affecting the International segment. Notes that U.S. dollar strength against the Canadian dollar negatively impacted revenue by approximately $26 million in Q2 and $72 million in the first six months of fiscal 2024. Mentions use of forward contracts to manage currency exposure.

## Item 4: Controls and Procedures
- Describes the company's disclosure controls and procedures designed to ensure timely and accurate reporting to the SEC
- Confirms that management, including the CEO and CFO, evaluated these controls as of July 29, 2023, and concluded they were effective
- Reports no material changes to internal control over financial reporting during the fiscal quarter

## Item 1: Legal Proceedings
- Brief reference directing readers to Note 10 (Contingencies) in the financial statements for details on legal proceedings

structure.title: Part II — Other Information
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document from Best Buy Co., Inc.'s Form 10-Q for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 covers the following main points:

## Item 3: Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk
- **Interest Rate Risk**: The company has $1.5 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $0.5 billion in floating-rate debt. A 50-basis point change in interest rates would impact net interest income by approximately $5 million.
- **Foreign Currency Exchange Rate Risk**: The company uses foreign exchange forward contracts to manage exposure related to international operations. Strength of the U.S. dollar against the Canadian dollar negatively impacted revenue by approximately $26 million in Q2 and $72 million in the first six months of fiscal 2024.

## Item 4: Controls and Procedures
- The company maintains disclosure controls and procedures designed to ensure timely and accurate reporting to the SEC.
- Management, including the CEO and CFO, evaluated and concluded that disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of July 29, 2023.
- No material changes to internal control over financial reporting occurred during the fiscal quarter.

## Item 2: Stock Repurchases
- The Board approved a $5.0 billion share repurchase program on February 28, 2022 with no expiration date.
- In Q2 fiscal 2024, the company repurchased 896,277 shares at an average price of $76.88 per share.
- Approximately $3.975 billion remained available for repurchase under the program.

## Item 1, 5, and 6: Legal Proceedings, Rule 10b5-1 Plans, and Exhibits
- References to contingencies and exhibits included in the quarterly report filing.

nodes.title: Item 1. Legal Proceedings
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document from a Form 10-Q filing covers the following main points:

## Item 3: Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk
- **Interest Rate Risk**: The company has $1.5 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $0.5 billion in floating-rate debt, resulting in a net $1.0 billion exposure to interest rate changes. A 50-basis point rate change would impact interest income by approximately $5 million.
- **Foreign Currency Exchange Rate Risk**: The company uses foreign exchange forward contracts to manage exposure related to international operations. In fiscal 2024's second quarter and first six months, U.S. dollar strength against the Canadian dollar negatively impacted revenue by approximately $26 million and $72 million, respectively.

## Item 4: Controls and Procedures
- The company maintains disclosure controls and procedures designed to ensure timely and accurate reporting to the SEC.
- A Disclosure Committee meets quarterly to evaluate effectiveness.
- Management, including the CEO and CFO, concluded that disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of July 29, 2023.
- No material changes to internal control over financial reporting occurred during the fiscal quarter.

## Item 1: Legal Proceedings
- References contingencies disclosed in the Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements.

## Item 2: Unregistered Sales of Equity Securities and Stock Repurchases
- Details a $5.0 billion share repurchase program approved February 28, 2022.
- Reports 896,277 shares repurchased in Q2 fiscal 2024 at an average price of $76.88 per share.
- Approximately $3.975 billion remains available under the repurchase authorization.

## Item 5 & 6: Other Information and Exhibits
- No Rule 10b5-1 trading plan changes during the quarter.
- Lists exhibits including corporate governance documents, officer certifications, and financial statements in iXBRL format.

# Description of Partial Document

structure.title: Signatures
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains the signature pages and certification exhibits from Best Buy Co., Inc.'s Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended July 29, 2023, filed on September 1, 2023.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Official Signatures**: Authorized signatures from three senior executives:
   - Corie Barry, Chief Executive Officer
   - Matthew Bilunas, Chief Financial Officer
   - Mathew R. Watson, Senior Vice President, Finance – Controller and Chief Accounting Officer

2. **Sarbanes-Oxley Section 302 Certifications (Exhibits 31.1 and 31.2)**: Certifications by the CEO and CFO confirming:
   - Accuracy and completeness of the quarterly report
   - Fair presentation of financial statements and financial condition
   - Responsibility for establishing and maintaining disclosure controls and procedures
   - Responsibility for internal control over financial reporting
   - Evaluation of control effectiveness
   - Disclosure of any material changes in internal controls
   - Disclosure of significant deficiencies, material weaknesses, and fraud to auditors and audit committee

3. **Sarbanes-Oxley Section 906 Certifications (Exhibits 32.1 and 32.2)**: Certifications by the CEO and CFO confirming:
   - Full compliance with Securities Exchange Act requirements
   - Fair presentation of financial condition and results of operations
   - Retention and availability of signed certification documents for SEC review
